long_description:
Reference: CS4424672 (now Sev 1 Case)
IKS worker (02k7_951ff839-79ee-43f0-91ca-6a62b0ea0605) is not able to communicate with master/ IKS backend.
short_description: Unable to create new worker in a worker pool
close_notes:
All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Brief Summary of the issue: IKS worker (02k7_951ff839-79ee-43f0-91ca-6a62b0ea0605) is not able to communicate with master/ IKS backend.
Impacted Env(s): BNPP_EU REGION
IPOps SME Escalated: 
Reason of escalation to Service Team: 
Cause Code:
Runbooks used to resolve: 
Error logs: https://github.ibm.com/cloudlab/ipops-vpc-cases/issues/7476
Timeline summary of actions done:
2025-12-09 17:04 Upgraded to sev 3 cie
2025-12-09 17:20 checking for any dropped packets. Upon checking, the communication between worker node and vpe looks fine. 
2025-12-09 17:23:03 customer stopped the instance
2025-12-09 17:46:03 Ipops joined the customer call with IKS team
2025-12-09 18:03 update from the call IKS are reviewing there network policies that were creted ~31 h ago and 80m ago
2025-12-09 18:11 Customer has deleted these globalnetworkpolicies
2025-12-09 18:56 Customer cordoned all the nodes and they are rebooting. Will check if calico-typha pods will be running fine on these and reachable to api server.
2025-12-09 19:10 paged sdn
2025-12-09 19:33 Checking networkpolicies and globallnetworkpolicies yaml file to see if it is correctly configured.
2025-12-09 19:51 Customer edited the networkpolicy. The ports for haproxy were changed from single line to multi-line format in the yaml file.
The nodes are uncordoned
The pods are running fine now.
The issue happened due to customer's misconfiguration in network policies. 
IKS team is reverting the changes made during investigation now. 
2025-12-09 20:10 downgraded to normal inc
Resolution steps:
Customer edited the networkpolicy. The ports for haproxy were changed from single line to multi-line format in the yaml file.
Warroom (if any): #warroom-vpc-inc10513395
Webex (recording if any): 
Customer Webex link (if any):
Caused by Change/Incident/Case/DevReq (if any): https://github.ibm.com/cloudlab/ipops-vpc-cases/issues/7476 - caused by customer's misconfiguration
Downstream Services impacted (if any): 
Gaps IPOps faced(if any):
Business impact for customer(if any): 
Jira's created (if any):
Emergency CR (if any):
PRB or pTasks(if any):
Collection of logs/Evidences for RCA:
Decision of Inc Status & Severity:
Detection time: 2025-12-09 16:25:00
Identification time: 
Mitigation time:
Outage start: 2025-12-08 23:58:00
Outage end: downgraded to normal inc
Potential Customer Accounts Impacted:
NEXT UPDATE TIME on Webex:
CIE pilot/co-pilot names:  Sudeep Nair, Sakshi Dhingra
CIE squad and Manager names:  John H, John DD, Joby George
current_status_next_steps:
Nodes have been successfully uncordoned.
All pods are running normally.
No service disruption observed at this time.

Next Steps:
IKS team is continuing to debug and validate the configuration changes.
Monitoring for any anomalies or regressions.
customer_facing_impact: - -User might experience issues creating new workers.
chronology:
Response Team Participants (add abbrev. role and name as needed)
=====================
SRE(s): Sudeep Nair, Justin McCright
DEV:
MGR: Johanna Cook, JOBY GEORGE (IaaS CORE)
iPOPs: John Haughton, John Diez-Daly 
AVM: Paul Stewart
IKS: Navid Gharib

#warroom-vpc-inc10513395 - https://ibm.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A2MHMPF3N
https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/21901539690652?p=ecrsevzvV0FHcojDgp 

Times in UTC
[YYYY-MMM-DD]
hh:mm AAA Impact Start
hh:mm AAA Sev# CIE Confirmed
hh:mm AAA <AVM Name> Joining as AVM OnDuty

17:08 BNPP case from last night with IKS now increased to Sev 1 45 mins ago. IKS worked not able to communicate with the master or the IKS back end
17:11 Sev 3 CIE is appropriate
17:20 seeing no dropped packets - definitely not a firewall issue
17:21 the TCP dump shows good connection with DNS and there is 2 way communications between worker node and VPE
17:23 looks like the issue is not at the IaaS end - need to get back to IKS. (IKS Navid Gharib is on a different call with the client)
17:29 it appears the customer has stopped the instance as of 17:23 UTC
17:39 the client are checking the cluster status after replaced a node and restarted the others
17:48 there appears to be no IaaS CIE. AVM manager recommended to keep the CIE in progress for a while until there is clarity from BNPP
17:48 moving to customer facing call 
https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetupjoin/19%3ameeting_YmY3NzZhNGEtNDVlMy00ODdkLTg2ZTE[…]2c%22Oid%22%3a%224022c602-1123-436a-a5a5-86a9440c1ed3%22%7d
18:29 The calico-node pods are not running as expected. Tried to delete the globalnetworkpolicies created 31h ago, but it didn't help.
We are investigating with customer and IKS team to find the root cause of issue.
18:30 The service team is attempting to create a connectivity agent (DAEMON SAT) using the host network instead of the cluster network, in order to check if we can at least retrieve logs.
18:38 DNS resolution is currently not working, and the team is investigating the root cause
20:33 Specifically, the ports for HAProxy were changed from a single-line format to a multi-line format in the YAML file, which caused connectivity issues.
Actions Taken:
Nodes were uncordoned.
Pods are now running normally.
IKS team is reverting temporary changes made during the investigation to restore the original configuration.
Root Cause:
Customer-edited network policy introduced incorrect port configuration.
Next Steps:
Complete rollback of investigation changes.
Monitor cluster stability.
=============================
               FOR AVM REFERENCE
=============================
